overview: A microservices-based leave management system with a React frontend (leave-portal) consuming a Go backend API (leave-service). The leave-service handles all business logic for leave requests, approvals, balances, and policies, exposing RESTful endpoints. All components run as Docker containers on Kubernetes with health checks.
requirements: Employees must be able to submit leave requests with date range and leave type
Managers must be able to view leave requests from their direct reports
Managers must be able to approve or deny leave requests
Employees must be able to view their current leave balance by leave type
HR admins must be able to set annual leave allocations for employees
All users must be able to view the status of their submitted leave requests
System must maintain leave types (annual, sick, personal, etc.)
System must track leave balances and deduct approved leave from balances
System must enforce manager-employee reporting hierarchy
All endpoints must include /health for readiness probes
API must validate date ranges and prevent overlapping leave requests
System must support role-based access control (Employee, Manager, HR Admin)

components.name: leave-service
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Leave Management API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: Backend API for WSO2 internal leave management system
servers:
  - url: http://localhost:9090
    description: Development server
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check endpoint
      operationId: healthCheck
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service is healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
  /api/v1/leave-requests:
    post:
      summary: Submit a new leave request
      operationId: createLeaveRequest
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - employeeId
                - leaveType
                - startDate
                - endDate
              properties:
                employeeId:
                  type: string
                leaveType:
                  type: string
                  enum: [annual, sick, personal, unpaid]
                startDate:
                  type: string
                  format: date
                endDate:
                  type: string
                  format: date
                reason:
                  type: string
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Leave request created
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
        '400':
          description: Invalid request (overlapping dates, insufficient balance)
        '500':
          description: Server error
    get:
      summary: List leave requests
      operationId: listLeaveRequests
      parameters:
        - name: employeeId
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
        - name: managerId
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
        - name: status
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
            enum: [pending, approved, denied]
      responses:
        '200':
          description: List of leave requests
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
  /api/v1/leave-requests/{id}:
    get:
      summary: Get a specific leave request
      operationId: getLeaveRequest
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Leave request details
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
        '404':
          description: Leave request not found
    patch:
      summary: Update leave request status (approve/deny)
      operationId: updateLeaveRequestStatus
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - status
                - reviewerId
              properties:
                status:
                  type: string
                  enum: [approved, denied]
                reviewerId:
                  type: string
                comments:
                  type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Leave request updated
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
        '403':
          description: Unauthorized (not the manager)
        '404':
          description: Leave request not found
  /api/v1/employees/{id}/balance:
    get:
      summary: Get employee leave balance
      operationId: getLeaveBalance
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Leave balance by type
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  employeeId:
                    type: string
                  balances:
                    type: array
                    items:
                      type: object
                      properties:
                        leaveType:
                          type: string
                        allocated:
                          type: number
                        used:
                          type: number
                        remaining:
                          type: number
        '404':
          description: Employee not found
    put:
      summary: Set employee annual leave allocation (HR admin only)
      operationId: setLeaveAllocation
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - leaveType
                - allocated
              properties:
                leaveType:
                  type: string
                allocated:
                  type: number
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Allocation updated
        '403':
          description: Unauthorized (not HR admin)
components:
  schemas:
    LeaveRequest:
      type: object
      properties:
        id:
          type: string
        employeeId:
          type: string
        leaveType:
          type: string
        startDate:
          type: string
          format: date
        endDate:
          type: string
          format: date
        reason:
          type: string
        status:
          type: string
          enum: [pending, approved, denied]
        reviewerId:
          type: string
        reviewerComments:
          type: string
        createdAt:
          type: string
          format: date-time
        updatedAt:
          type: string
          format: date-time
components.componentAgentInstructions: Implement a Go HTTP service using net/http listening on port 9090. Use an in-memory data store (maps with mutexes) for leave requests, employee balances, and employee-manager relationships. Implement all endpoints from the OpenAPI spec. For /health return {"status":"ok"}. For POST /api/v1/leave-requests, validate that startDate < endDate, check for overlapping requests for the same employee, verify sufficient leave balance, create a new request with status 'pending', deduct from balance only when approved. For GET /api/v1/leave-requests, filter by employeeId (returns user's own requests), managerId (returns requests from direct reports), or status. For PATCH /api/v1/leave-requests/{id}, verify the reviewerId is the employee's manager, update status to approved/denied, if approved deduct days from balance. For GET /api/v1/employees/{id}/balance, return all leave types with allocated/used/remaining counts. For PUT /api/v1/employees/{id}/balance, allow setting allocations (simulate HR admin check). Seed initial data: 3 employees (emp1, emp2, emp3), 1 manager (mgr1 manages emp1 and emp2), each employee starts with 20 annual leave days, 10 sick days. Use JSON for all request/response bodies. Handle errors with appropriate HTTP status codes. Use structured logging to stdout. Dockerfile: multi-stage build with golang:1.21 builder, final scratch image, EXPOSE 9090, CMD the binary.

components.name: leave-portal
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Leave Portal Frontend
  version: 1.0.0
  description: Static web application serving the leave management UI
servers:
  - url: http://localhost:3000
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Frontend is serving
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
components.componentAgentInstructions: Implement a React SPA using TypeScript and Vite. The app has three main views: (1) Employee Dashboard — shows current leave balance (fetch GET /api/v1/employees/{id}/balance from leave-service), lists user's leave requests (GET /api/v1/leave-requests?employeeId={id}), and a form to submit new leave requests (POST /api/v1/leave-requests with date pickers for start/end, dropdown for leave type). (2) Manager Dashboard — lists pending leave requests from direct reports (GET /api/v1/leave-requests?managerId={id}&status=pending), each request shows employee name, dates, type, reason, with Approve/Deny buttons (PATCH /api/v1/leave-requests/{id}). (3) HR Admin Panel — form to set leave allocations per employee (PUT /api/v1/employees/{id}/balance). Use React Router for navigation. Hard-code user role in localStorage (employee/manager/hradmin) and current userId for demo purposes. Style with Tailwind CSS. Use fetch API to call leave-service on http://leave-service:9090. Display error messages for failed requests (overlapping dates, insufficient balance). Implement a /health endpoint by serving a simple Express server on port 3000 that returns {"status":"ok"} and serves the built React app from /dist. Dockerfile: Node 18 builder (npm install, npm run build), then Node 18 runtime with express serving static files and /health, EXPOSE 3000, CMD node server.js. Create server.js: const express = require('express'); const path = require('path'); const app = express(); app.get('/health', (req, res) => res.json({status: 'ok'})); app.use(express.static(path.join(__dirname, 'dist'))); app.get('*', (req, res) => res.sendFile(path.join(__dirname, 'dist', 'index.html'))); app.listen(3000, () => console.log('Server running on port 3000'));
